message: Fair bass conditions right now. Could go either way.
positive_factors: Stable atmospheric pressure is comfortable for fish
A light breeze/ripple has a very favorable effect
negative_factors: Bright, clear skies can push fish deep and slow feeding

tactics.where_reason: fish push shallow toward the bank in low light / cool water
tactics.method_reason: conditions don't strongly favor one over the other

fly_options.name: Poppers / foam topwater
fly_options.why: low light + warm water = surface bass eats

fly_options.name: Dry flies (caddis / mayfly)
fly_options.why: trout rise to the evening hatch in low light

fly_options.name: Woolly Buggers / leeches (slow strip)
fly_options.why: all-purpose in low light, wind, or stained water — both species

fly_options.name: Streamers (sculpin / baitfish)
fly_options.why: active fish chasing bait; strip on a sink-tip

spin_options.name: Topwater (buzzbaits, walking baits, poppers)
spin_options.why: first/last light over shallow cover for bass

spin_options.name: Spinners / spinnerbaits
spin_options.why: chop or cloud cover — cover water at mid-depth

spin_options.name: Jigs & soft plastics (worked deep)
spin_options.why: bright sun / warm water — fish off the bank and on structure

tactics.season_note: Summer: bass prime in warm shallows early/late; trout slide deep and cool midday.
tactics.summary: Largemouth bass · Shoreline & shallow cover · Shallow — top ~0–8 ft · lean either — match the water
